subject: History
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse how historians construct knowledge about the past
Evaluate the role and limitations of dates in historical study
Critically examine the concept of periodisation
Assess James Mill’s interpretation of Indian history
Interpret different types of historical sources
Understand how colonial administration produced historical records

lesson_plan.title: Nature and Scope of History
student_activities: Identify examples of historical change in society
Discuss how past events shape present-day institutions

lesson_plan.title: Role and Limitations of Dates
topics_covered: Importance of dates in organising historical events
Limitations of focusing only on dates
student_activities: Construct a timeline of selected historical events
Analyse why some events become historically significant

lesson_plan.title: Concept of Periodisation
student_activities: Compare different periodisations used by historians
Debate the usefulness of dividing history into periods

lesson_plan.title: James Mill’s Interpretation of Indian History
topics_covered: Influence of colonial thinking on historical writing
student_activities: Analyse the strengths and weaknesses of Mill’s classification
Write a short paragraph explaining why historians criticize it

lesson_plan.title: Historical Sources and Evidence
topics_covered: Importance of evidence in historical interpretation
student_activities: Examine examples of historical sources such as letters or reports
Explain what information historians can obtain from them

lesson_plan.title: Colonial Records and Chapter Review
topics_covered: Importance of archives in historical research
student_activities: Participate in a critical thinking quiz about the chapter

teaching_aids: Copies of historical documents or images
teacher_tip: Encourage students to question historical interpretations and analyse sources critically so they develop deeper historical reasoning skills.
